key_insight: AutoDFT closes the gap between planning and execution in DFT by using a multi-agent loop that can adapt parameters, recover from failures, and revise plans as calculations evolve.
engineering_relevance: This makes first-principles simulations more robust and accessible for materials and chemistry workflows, reducing the need for expert intervention when runs stall or intermediate results change the path.
method: Strategic planner creates a skeletal DFT workflow with step objectives.
Step planner generates numerical parameters just in time from prior results.
Monitor-recover-reflect loop detects failures, repairs them, and updates the plan when needed.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 94.1% task-level success on VASPBench with GPT-5.2 across 34 tasks and 9 DFT calculation types, and states that results are quantitatively reliable on established materials databases.
watch_item: Performance depends on LLM-driven control and careful integration with DFT software, so robustness may vary across codes, task types, and failure modes.
paper_title: AutoDFT: A Closed-Loop Multi-Agent Framework for Autonomous DFT Calculations
